steps.title: Identify the tension between the two texts
steps.content: Text 1 frames Orlando as an oddity — fantastical, psychologically superficial, even dismissed by Woolf herself as “a joke.” Text 2 counters that Orlando shares the same core themes as Woolf’s other classics, making it central to her body of work despite its lighter tone. The author of Text 2 would acknowledge the difference but push back on the “minor work” label.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice and pick the match
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘By conceding that Woolf's talents were best suited to serious novels but asserting that the humor in Orlando is often effective’: Text 2 never says Woolf’s talents were best suited to serious novels — it puts Orlando on equal thematic footing. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘By agreeing that Orlando is less impressive than certain other novels by Woolf but arguing that it should still be regarded as a classic’: Text 2 does not call Orlando less impressive; it argues it engages the same themes as the classics. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘By acknowledging that Orlando clearly differs from Woolf's other major novels but insisting on its centrality to her body of work nonetheless’: Text 2 concedes the lighter tone yet insists the novel engages the same serious themes — a perfect match. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘By concurring that the reputation of Orlando as a minor work has led readers to overlook this novel but maintaining that the reputation is unearned’: Neither text discusses readers overlooking Orlando. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
